# Turtleand AI Lab

> A hands-on, 6-module curriculum for building practical LLM tools and agents. Self-paced, free, and open.

## Curriculum

### Beginner doorway
- [AI for Beginners](https://lab.turtleand.com/beginners/): A zero-start doorway into Turtleand AI Lab that explains AI in plain language while keeping human judgment at the center.
- [Simple AI, for beginners](https://lab.turtleand.com/beginners/topics/simple-ai/): A plain-language doorway into what AI is, what it is not, and how to use it without giving away judgment.
- [Start safely with AI tools](https://lab.turtleand.com/beginners/topics/start-safely-with-ai/): A beginner safety baseline for using AI tools without giving away private data, judgment, or responsibility.
- [Access and secrets, for beginners](https://lab.turtleand.com/beginners/topics/access-secrets/): A beginner explanation of API keys, passwords, environment variables, and why secrets should not be pasted into AI tools.
- [Running AI locally, for beginners](https://lab.turtleand.com/beginners/topics/running-inference-locally/): A plain-language first path for understanding local AI inference before reading the full technical guide.
- [Agent notifications, for beginners](https://lab.turtleand.com/beginners/topics/agent-notifications/): A beginner guide to why agents should notify humans when work finishes, blocks, or needs approval.
- [Prompt mastery, for beginners](https://lab.turtleand.com/beginners/topics/prompt-mastery/): A simple path from vague requests to useful prompts with goals, context, constraints, examples, and review.
- [Multi-tool AI workflows, for beginners](https://lab.turtleand.com/beginners/topics/multi-tool-workflows/): How to choose the right AI tool for each step instead of forcing one chat box to do everything.
- [Model selection and economics, for beginners](https://lab.turtleand.com/beginners/topics/model-selection-economics/): A beginner way to choose between fast, cheap, capable, and careful models for the task in front of you.
- [Voice and multimodal workflows, for beginners](https://lab.turtleand.com/beginners/topics/voice-multimodal-workflows/): How voice, images, screenshots, and audio can make AI more useful when text is not enough.
- [Human-AI collaboration, for beginners](https://lab.turtleand.com/beginners/topics/human-ai-collaboration-handbook/): A beginner handbook for working with AI as a collaborator while keeping human direction and taste.
- [Automation pipelines, for beginners](https://lab.turtleand.com/beginners/topics/automation-pipelines/): A beginner way to turn repeated work into checked steps before adding automation.
- [AI-first lifestyle, for beginners](https://lab.turtleand.com/beginners/topics/ai-first-lifestyle/): How to build daily AI habits that prepare work, clarify thinking, and still leave decisions to the human.
- [MCP servers, for beginners](https://lab.turtleand.com/beginners/topics/building-mcp-server/): A plain explanation of how an MCP server lets an AI tool read from or act through a controlled interface.
- [Living institutional memory, for beginners](https://lab.turtleand.com/beginners/topics/living-institutional-memory/): How notes, decisions, lessons, and public artifacts can become a memory system that compounds.
- [Persistent agents, for beginners](https://lab.turtleand.com/beginners/topics/persistent-agents/): A beginner explanation of agents that keep context, run over time, and need clear boundaries.
- [Context is not memory, for beginners](https://lab.turtleand.com/beginners/topics/context-is-not-memory/): A beginner explanation of the difference between what a model sees now and what a system remembers later.
- [Planning and decomposition, for beginners](https://lab.turtleand.com/beginners/topics/planning-decomposition-control-structures/): How to make AI work better by splitting a goal into smaller steps with checks between them.
- [The agent loop, for beginners](https://lab.turtleand.com/beginners/topics/agent-loop-controlled-feedback-loop/): A plain-language view of how agents observe, choose an action, inspect the result, and repeat safely.
- [Agent tool use, for beginners](https://lab.turtleand.com/beginners/topics/agent-tool-use-controlled-action-cycle/): How tools give agents hands and senses while still needing scope, checks, and human approval at risky boundaries.
- [Agentic AI systems, for beginners](https://lab.turtleand.com/beginners/topics/agentic-ai-systems-learning-path/): A beginner map of the ideas behind agents: loop, tools, memory, planning, approval, reliability, trust infrastructure, and coordination.
- [Human agency is not a button, for beginners](https://lab.turtleand.com/beginners/topics/human-agency-is-not-a-button/): Why responsible agent systems need real human authority, not just a decorative approval prompt.
- [Smart is not reliable, for beginners](https://lab.turtleand.com/beginners/topics/smart-is-not-reliable/): Why impressive AI answers are not the same as dependable systems.
- [Trust through evidence, for beginners](https://lab.turtleand.com/beginners/topics/trust-is-earned-through-evidence-not-vibes/): A plain-language guide to trusting agents only when they can show what happened, what was checked, and when humans must decide.
- [Model choice is system design, for beginners](https://lab.turtleand.com/beginners/topics/model-choice-is-system-design/): A beginner guide to choosing the right model route for the task, risk, cost, and human decision boundary.
- [More agents, more coordination, for beginners](https://lab.turtleand.com/beginners/topics/more-agents-more-coordination/): A beginner guide to why adding more agents creates coordination work, not automatic intelligence.

### Spanish beginner doorway
- [IA para principiantes](https://lab.turtleand.com/es/beginners/): La puerta en lenguaje claro hacia AI Lab para personas que empiezan desde cero.
- [IA simple, para principiantes](https://lab.turtleand.com/es/beginners/topics/simple-ai/): Una puerta en lenguaje claro para entender qué es la IA, qué no es y cómo usarla sin entregar el juicio.
- [Empezar con seguridad con herramientas de IA](https://lab.turtleand.com/es/beginners/topics/start-safely-with-ai/): Una base de seguridad para usar IA sin entregar datos privados, juicio o responsabilidad.
- [Accesos y secretos, para principiantes](https://lab.turtleand.com/es/beginners/topics/access-secrets/): Una explicación simple de API keys, contraseñas, variables de entorno y por qué no se pegan secretos en herramientas de IA.
- [Ejecutar IA localmente, para principiantes](https://lab.turtleand.com/es/beginners/topics/running-inference-locally/): Una primera ruta en lenguaje simple para entender inferencia local antes de leer la guía técnica completa.
- [Notificaciones de agentes, para principiantes](https://lab.turtleand.com/es/beginners/topics/agent-notifications/): Por qué los agentes deberían avisar cuando terminan, se bloquean o necesitan aprobación humana.
- [Dominio de prompts, para principiantes](https://lab.turtleand.com/es/beginners/topics/prompt-mastery/): Una ruta simple desde pedidos vagos hacia prompts útiles con objetivo, contexto, límites, ejemplos y revisión.
- [Workflows multi-herramienta, para principiantes](https://lab.turtleand.com/es/beginners/topics/multi-tool-workflows/): Cómo elegir la herramienta adecuada para cada paso en vez de forzar una sola caja de chat a hacer todo.
- [Selección de modelos y economía, para principiantes](https://lab.turtleand.com/es/beginners/topics/model-selection-economics/): Una forma principiante de elegir entre modelos rápidos, baratos, capaces y cuidadosos según la tarea.
- [Voz y workflows multimodales, para principiantes](https://lab.turtleand.com/es/beginners/topics/voice-multimodal-workflows/): Cómo voz, imágenes, capturas y audio pueden hacer más útil la IA cuando el texto no alcanza.
- [Colaboración humano-IA, para principiantes](https://lab.turtleand.com/es/beginners/topics/human-ai-collaboration-handbook/): Una guía para trabajar con IA como colaboradora manteniendo dirección y criterio humano.
- [Pipelines de automatización, para principiantes](https://lab.turtleand.com/es/beginners/topics/automation-pipelines/): Una forma principiante de convertir trabajo repetido en pasos revisados antes de automatizar.
- [Estilo de vida AI-first, para principiantes](https://lab.turtleand.com/es/beginners/topics/ai-first-lifestyle/): Cómo construir hábitos diarios con IA que preparan trabajo y aclaran pensamiento sin entregar decisiones.
- [Servidores MCP, para principiantes](https://lab.turtleand.com/es/beginners/topics/building-mcp-server/): Una explicación simple de cómo un servidor MCP permite que una herramienta de IA lea o actúe mediante una interfaz controlada.
- [Memoria institucional viva, para principiantes](https://lab.turtleand.com/es/beginners/topics/living-institutional-memory/): Cómo notas, decisiones, lecciones y artefactos públicos pueden convertirse en un sistema de memoria que compone.
- [Agentes persistentes, para principiantes](https://lab.turtleand.com/es/beginners/topics/persistent-agents/): Una explicación de agentes que conservan contexto, corren en el tiempo y necesitan límites claros.
- [Contexto no es memoria, para principiantes](https://lab.turtleand.com/es/beginners/topics/context-is-not-memory/): La diferencia entre lo que un modelo ve ahora y lo que un sistema recuerda después.
- [Planificación y descomposición, para principiantes](https://lab.turtleand.com/es/beginners/topics/planning-decomposition-control-structures/): Cómo hacer que la IA trabaje mejor dividiendo un objetivo en pasos chicos con controles entre medio.
- [El loop de agente, para principiantes](https://lab.turtleand.com/es/beginners/topics/agent-loop-controlled-feedback-loop/): Cómo los agentes observan, eligen una acción, revisan el resultado y repiten con seguridad.
- [Uso de herramientas por agentes, para principiantes](https://lab.turtleand.com/es/beginners/topics/agent-tool-use-controlled-action-cycle/): Cómo las herramientas les dan manos y sentidos a los agentes sin quitar la necesidad de alcance, chequeos y aprobación humana en límites riesgosos.
- [Sistemas agentic de IA, para principiantes](https://lab.turtleand.com/es/beginners/topics/agentic-ai-systems-learning-path/): Un mapa principiante de agentes: loop, herramientas, memoria, planificación, aprobación, confiabilidad, infraestructura de confianza y coordinación.
- [La agencia humana no es un botón, para principiantes](https://lab.turtleand.com/es/beginners/topics/human-agency-is-not-a-button/): Por qué los sistemas de agentes responsables necesitan autoridad humana real, no solo un prompt decorativo de aprobación.
- [Inteligente no es confiable, para principiantes](https://lab.turtleand.com/es/beginners/topics/smart-is-not-reliable/): Por qué respuestas impresionantes de IA no son lo mismo que sistemas confiables.
- [Confianza con evidencia, para principiantes](https://lab.turtleand.com/es/beginners/topics/trust-is-earned-through-evidence-not-vibes/): Una guía simple para confiar en agentes solo cuando pueden mostrar qué pasó, qué se chequeó y cuándo decide una persona.
- [Elegir modelo es diseñar el sistema, para principiantes](https://lab.turtleand.com/es/beginners/topics/model-choice-is-system-design/): Una guía principiante para elegir la ruta de modelo correcta según tarea, riesgo, costo y límite de decisión humana.
- [Más agentes, más coordinación, para principiantes](https://lab.turtleand.com/es/beginners/topics/more-agents-more-coordination/): Una guía principiante sobre por qué agregar agentes crea trabajo de coordinación, no inteligencia automática.

### Module 0: Fast Track Setup
- [Access & secrets](https://lab.turtleand.com/topics/access-secrets): Configure API keys and secrets securely for model access.
- [Safety baseline](https://lab.turtleand.com/topics/safety-baseline): Practical safety for AI infrastructure — firewalls, audits, and access control.
- [Simple AI](https://lab.turtleand.com/topics/simple-ai): A beginner doorway for understanding what AI is, what it is not, and where to go next inside Turtleand.

### Module 0: Setup & Safety
- [Agent notifications](https://lab.turtleand.com/topics/agent-notifications): Get notified when your agent finishes using hooks and ntfy.
- [Running inference locally](https://lab.turtleand.com/topics/running-inference-locally): Easily setup & run an LLM on your own machine.

### Module 1: AI Power User
- [Human-AI collaboration handbook](https://lab.turtleand.com/topics/human-ai-collaboration-handbook): A quick-reference guide to principles for human-AI collaboration, from mindset to workflow.
- [Model selection & economics](https://lab.turtleand.com/topics/model-selection-economics): When to use Opus vs Sonnet, quota management, and cost-per-output thinking.
- [Multi-tool AI workflows](https://lab.turtleand.com/topics/multi-tool-workflows): Running 3+ AI tools in parallel, choosing the right tool for each task.
- [Prompt mastery](https://lab.turtleand.com/topics/prompt-mastery): Beyond basics — structured prompting for autonomous systems.
- [Voice & multimodal workflows](https://lab.turtleand.com/topics/voice-multimodal-workflows): Voice-to-AI pipelines, TTS output, and audio as a primary instruction method.

### Module 2: AI Integration & Orchestration
- [AI-first lifestyle](https://lab.turtleand.com/topics/ai-first-lifestyle): Morning briefings, memory systems, and making AI work while you sleep.
- [Automation pipelines](https://lab.turtleand.com/topics/automation-pipelines): Cron-driven AI workflows, multi-stage processing, and overnight analysis.
- [How I built a knowledge-base MCP server for my articles](https://lab.turtleand.com/topics/building-mcp-server): I built an MCP server that exposes 51 articles to any AI client. Two tools, zero config, no API keys. Here's how it works.
- [Living institutional memory](https://lab.turtleand.com/topics/living-institutional-memory): How organizations can use AI to ensure no knowledge ever degrades and no lesson is ever relearned.
- [From OpenClaw to Hermes](https://lab.turtleand.com/topics/persistent-agents): Why persistent-agent experiments graduated into a leaner, human-centered Hermes operating layer.

### Module 3: Builder Foundations
- [Context is not memory](https://lab.turtleand.com/topics/context-is-not-memory): Why agents need curated memory, not just bigger context windows.
- [Planning and decomposition as control structures for agents](https://lab.turtleand.com/topics/planning-decomposition-control-structures): How plans keep agent work bounded, observable, and adaptable.

### Module 5: Agent Architecture
- [The agent loop as a controlled feedback loop](https://lab.turtleand.com/topics/agent-loop-controlled-feedback-loop): A beginner lesson on how useful AI agents observe state, choose bounded actions, inspect results, and keep humans responsible for risky steps.
- [Agent tool use as a controlled action cycle](https://lab.turtleand.com/topics/agent-tool-use-controlled-action-cycle): A beginner lesson on how tools give agents hands and senses, why tool calls need permission boundaries, and how results should be checked before the next action.
- [Agentic AI systems learning path](https://lab.turtleand.com/topics/agentic-ai-systems-learning-path): A concise learning map for agents: loop, tools, memory, planning, approval, reliability, trust infrastructure, routing, and coordination.
- [Human Agency Is Not a Button](https://lab.turtleand.com/topics/human-agency-is-not-a-button): A beginner lesson on why responsible agent systems need authority boundaries, not just approval prompts.
- [Model Choice Is System Design](https://lab.turtleand.com/topics/model-choice-is-system-design): How agent systems choose models by task difficulty, risk, cost, latency, privacy, verification needs, and human authority boundaries.
- [More Agents, More Coordination](https://lab.turtleand.com/topics/more-agents-more-coordination): Why multi-agent systems trade individual workload for delegation, evidence, conflict resolution, and synthesis work.
- [Smart Is Not Reliable](https://lab.turtleand.com/topics/smart-is-not-reliable): Why agent reliability is a system property built from checks, constraints, recovery paths, observability, and human escalation.
- [Trust Is Earned Through Evidence, Not Vibes](https://lab.turtleand.com/topics/trust-is-earned-through-evidence-not-vibes): How agent systems earn trust through scoped tasks, permission boundaries, observability, evaluations, fallback paths, and independent verification.

## About
Created by Turtleand, software engineer building AI education that's practical, not theoretical.
- Portal: https://turtleand.com
- GitHub: https://github.com/turtleand
